summary: Added the decompiled Scizor/Hassam item implementation in src/melee/it/items/ithassam.c, including its state table, behavior callbacks, targeting/movement logic, camera-directed behavior, and item variable storage. The PR also promoted needed declarations in camera.h and it_2725.h, added HassamVars to common item types, wired it into Item::xDD4_itemVar, and changed many ithassam-local functions/constants in symbols.txt from global to local. Author notes say it_802CE400 was the only non-matching part and that inlines in it_802CE0C4 and it_802CE400 were omitted due to stack usage.
what_changed: Implemented src/melee/it/items/ithassam.c, expanding it from an empty/stub file to roughly 378 lines of C for Hassam item behavior.
Added static prototypes for most internal ithassam functions from it_802CDC5C through it_802CE6E8 and defined the ItemStateTable it_803F7CF8 with four states.
Implemented initialization and lifecycle functions such as it_802CDBE0, it_802CDC60, it_802CDC80, it_802CDCB4, and state callbacks for animation, physics, collision, and transition handling.

Implemented targeting logic in it_802CDE1C that scans HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters, filters invalid/team cases with ftLib/gm helpers, chooses the nearest fighter above the item position, and falls back to ftLib_8008627C if no suitable target is found.

Implemented movement setup in it_802CDF28 using ftLib_800866DC, lbVector_Diff, lbVector_Rotate, atan2f, and HassamVars attributes to compute stored movement vector xDD4_itemVar.hassam.x5C and item velocity.

Implemented state transition behavior around it_802CE008, it_802CE0C4, it_802CE308, and it_802CE3A8, including life timer usage, facing direction changes, rotation over attr->x20 frames, hitlag pause/resume callbacks, and velocity accumulation.

Implemented it_802CE400 as a known non-matching function that switches to animation state 2, queries camera position with Camera_80030788, normalizes a vector toward the camera, stores scaled movement in HassamVars, clears velocity/facing, and resets Y rotation.
Added HassamVars to src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h with f32/s32 fields, padding to offset 0x5C, a Vec3 at x5C, and an f32 at x68.
Added HassamVars hassam to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Added required external declarations: Camera_80030788(Vec3*) in camera.h, it_80272980(Item_GObj*, f32), and it_8027A13C(Item_GObj*) in it_2725.h.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark many ithassam functions and related data/constants as local rather than global, including it_802CDC5C, it_802CDC80 through it_802CE6E8, it_803F7D38, it_804D5600, it_804D5608, and several sdata2 constants.
smart_moves: Kept internal item callbacks static and reflected that in symbols.txt scope changes, reducing accidental global API surface for item-local behavior.
Added only the header declarations needed by the new decompilation instead of broad placeholder usage, including specific prototypes for Camera_80030788 and item helper functions.
Modeled per-item scratch state with a named HassamVars struct and added it to Item::xDD4_itemVar, making repeated xDD4 field access clearer than raw offsets.
Preserved matching-sensitive details with PAD_STACK calls and explicit author notes about omitted inlines where stack usage affected matching.
Used the item state table pattern with animation/physics/collision callbacks, matching common Melee item decomp organization and making behavior phases searchable.
Localized special-attribute access through Article::x4_specialAttributes cast to HassamVars, which documents that these fields are article-specific parameters rather than generic item state.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling item files, convert private callbacks to static and update symbols.txt from global to local for the same address range.
For item-specific xDD4 storage, add a named vars struct to itCommonItems.h and a corresponding union member in Item::xDD4_itemVar rather than leaving accesses as opaque padding.
If a matching function depends on inlines, omitting inlines can be a valid tactic when stack layout/usage is otherwise wrong; document the affected functions explicitly.
Camera-dependent item behavior may require exposing camera helper prototypes in cm/camera.h; here Camera_80030788(Vec3*) was needed to compute a vector from the item to the camera.
State table-driven item behavior should keep callback signatures consistent: bool for anim/collision-like callbacks and void for physics/state update callbacks, as seen in it_803F7CF8.
Target-selection code should preserve gameplay filters from the original assembly, including invalid-target checks, team checks, and fallback target helper behavior.
naming_conventions: The item file and PR use the Japanese/Pokémon internal name 'hassam' for Scizor, e.g. ithassam.c and HassamVars.
Unknown or not-yet-renamed functions retain address-based names such as it_802CE0C4 and it_802CE400.
Item-specific variable structs follow the pattern <ItemName>Vars, as with existing FFlowerVars and new HassamVars.
Item xDD4 union members use lowercase item names, e.g. xDD4_itemVar.hassam.
Struct fields remain offset-style names where semantics are not yet identified, such as HassamVars x0, x4, x8, x18, x5C, and x68.
Header prototypes include address comments matching existing project style, such as /* 030788 */ void Camera_80030788(Vec3*); and /* 27A13C */ void it_8027A13C(Item_GObj*);
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(12) in it_802CDBE0 and it_802CDC80 and PAD_STACK(8) in it_802CDE1C to preserve stack layout.
Author explicitly noted that inlines in it_802CE0C4 and it_802CE400 were omitted due to stack usage.
Author explicitly noted that it_802CE400 was the only non-matching part of the PR.
Changed symbol scopes for private functions/data from global to local after implementing static functions and file-local data.
Preserved explicit float operations and order in vector math, including separate assignment to x/y/z components and explicit zeroing of velocity/facing in it_802CE400.
Used direct gobj->user_data casts in some functions and GET_ITEM macros in others, suggesting expression form was chosen to satisfy local matching constraints.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text was the author note: it_802CE400 is non-matching, and inlines in it_802CE0C4 and it_802CE400 were omitted due to stack usage.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that make it_802CE400 matching or rename ithassam functions.
Search for additional item PRs that add Item::xDD4_itemVar union members and compare struct padding conventions.
Search for uses of Camera_80030788(Vec3*) to identify its real semantic name.
Search for later review or cleanup around omitted inlines in it_802CE0C4 and it_802CE400.
Compare ithassam state table patterns with other common item decomp PRs using ItemStateTable.
